symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid expansion of thick erythematous scaly plaques over trunk and extensor surfaces

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid expansion of characteristic thick scaly plaques in known psoriasis is the hallmark of a severe flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful fissuring and bleeding of plaques with serosanguinous exudate

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fissuring of thick plaques is a marker of severe, poorly controlled plaque disease.
Open fissures breach the barrier and create a portal for secondary bacterial infection — a risk factor, not yet an active infection.
Extensive active psoriasis (~35% BSA) carries a systemic inflammatory burden that produces fatigue and malaise; here it tracks the flare rather than an independent cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt cessation of high-potency topical corticosteroid three days prior

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Undertreatment/withdrawal of topical steroid is a common precipitant of a plaque psoriasis flare.
Abrupt corticosteroid withdrawal is a recognised trigger for transformation to generalized pustular (or erythrodermic) psoriasis — mandates active screening.
Psychological stress is a well-recognised, if non-specific, trigger for psoriasis flares.
High fever typically accompanies generalized pustular psoriasis; an afebrile patient argues against systemic pustular disease at this point.
Absence of fever/rigors makes established cellulitis with systemic upset or bacteraemia less likely now.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of visible sterile pustules or lakes of pus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Widespread sterile pustules coalescing into lakes of pus define generalized pustular psoriasis; their absence on inspection argues strongly against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetric distribution without a single unilateral hot spreading area

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cellulitis is typically a unilateral, single, rapidly expanding hot area; diffuse bilateral symmetric erythema fits the psoriasis flare, not cellulitis.
Symmetric extensor-surface and trunk involvement is the typical distribution of plaque psoriasis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of punched-out painful vesicles or disproportionate deterioration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Monomorphic punched-out vesicles and rapid disproportionate deterioration characterise eczema herpeticum; their absence makes it unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Extensive plaque coverage (approx 35% BSA) on clinician estimate

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ~35% BSA involvement marks severe disease and supports dermatology referral for systemic therapy; a formal BSA estimate needs an in-person exam and is sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No active synovitis on joint examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of active synovitis on the in-person joint exam argues against a concurrent psoriatic arthritis flare; this is a clinician examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fluctuance, honey-coloured crusting or red streaking on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fluctuance, honey crust or lymphangitic streaking on the in-person exam argues against abscess, impetiginisation or cellulitis; this is a clinician examination finding, sealed.
Absence of purulence/crusting on exam makes active secondary infection unlikely at this point.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rapid symmetric plaque expansion with fissuring, PLUS an afebrile state, no sterile pustules and no unilateral hot spreading area, together identify a severe plaque flare while screening the two emergent mimics (generalized pustular psoriasis, cellulitis) negative — the exact pattern that should keep triage at prompt-GP rather than ED.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab / culture of fissured skin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated now — there are no signs of secondary infection (afebrile, no purulence, no honey crust). Reserve for if a fissure becomes purulent, hot or spreading; ordering routinely is over-investigation and is an in-person action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and inflammatory markers
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Cannot be obtained via telehealth and not required to manage a clinically evident flare with a negative acute screen; appropriate only if systemic infection is suspected at in-person review.
